codeReview.summary: The reports.md documentation packet is thorough, well-structured, and well-grounded in actual source code. All 7 required report types (trial balance, P&L, balance sheet, cash flow, GST, aging, dashboards) are covered with explicit snapshot-vs-live and classification limitations. Cross-references are valid and the document is discoverable from MODULE-INVENTORY.md and INDEX.md. Three minor issues noted below (one undocumented endpoint, one incorrect cross-reference, one unverified claim about hierarchical P&L), none of which are blocking for VAL-FIN-002 compliance.

issues.description: Undocumented endpoint: `GET /api/v1/reports/inventory-reconciliation` exists in ReportController.java (line 131) but is not mentioned anywhere in the reports.md documentation packet.

Broken cross-reference: The 'Related Documentation' section links to `docs/workflows/accounting-and-period-close.md`, but this file does not exist. The `docs/workflows/` directory contains only `FLOW-INVENTORY.md`. The period close workflow is likely covered in `docs/developer/accounting-flows/08-period-close-reconciliation.md` or `docs/flows/FLOW-INVENTORY.md`.

Known Gaps section states 'No hierarchical P&L variant' but `ReportController.java` line 205 exposes `GET /api/v1/reports/income-statement/hierarchy` which appears to be exactly that. Either the endpoint is functional and should be documented, or it should be listed as a deprecated/dead surface.

summary: PASS. The docs-finance-reporting-truth feature produces a comprehensive, well-grounded reports module packet at docs/modules/reports.md. All seven report categories required by VAL-FIN-002 (trial balance, P&L, balance sheet, cash flow, GST, aging, dashboards) are documented with explicit source-of-truth behavior and snapshot-vs-live/classification limitations. The documentation references real controllers, services, DTOs, and repositories that all exist in the codebase. Freshness marker (Last reviewed: 2026-03-30) is present. The document is discoverable from both MODULE-INVENTORY.md and INDEX.md. Three non-blocking issues were found: (1) the inventory-reconciliation endpoint is undocumented, (2) a cross-reference to a non-existent workflows file, and (3) a claim about no hierarchical P&L despite an existing endpoint. None of these affect VAL-FIN-002 compliance.
